response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned engineer with strong systems and web experience, but his profile diverges from this role's core requirements. His C++ work is over a decade old (National Instruments, 2014–2015) and his recent career centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang rather than modern C++/STL. He shows adjacent strengths in low-latency, high-throughput, event-driven systems, but has no evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL practices, MQTT, or the listed industrial protocols, which are central to this position.
strengths: Deep experience with low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems (DLang, async I/O, fibers at Dunnhumby)
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source leadership
Broad cross-domain range spanning embedded, desktop, web, and geo-data platforms
concerns: C++ experience is dated (~2015) and brief relative to the role's C++-centric focus
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or OT protocol experience
Recent trajectory is full-stack web/AI tooling, suggesting misalignment with an on-prem industrial C++ role

key_factors.factor: Outdated C++ depth vs. core requirement
